skill.name: caveman-help
skill.description: Quick-reference card for all caveman modes, skills, and commands. One-shot display, not a persistent mode. Trigger: /caveman-help, "caveman help", "what caveman commands", "how do I use caveman".
skill.summary: Quick-reference card for all caveman modes, skills, and commands. One-shot display, not a persistent...
skill.author: juliusbrussee

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown backticks, a home-directory config example, a documentation URL, and weak-crypto keywords. Manual review found only a reference card in SKILL.md, with no executable code, network calls, secret access, or prompt injection attempt. The skill is safe to publish with low risk because the flagged patterns are false positives or benign documentation.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Flagged as Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The backtick detections are markdown examples of slash commands and shell snippets shown to the user. SKILL.md contains reference text only and does not execute Ruby, shell, or any other external command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are markdown table entries, inline command examples, and fenced examples. There is no script file, interpreter directive, or executable wrapper in the skill.

low_findings.title: Home Directory Config Path Is Documentation
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The hidden home-directory path is presented as a user configuration location. The skill instructs the assistant to display the reference card and explicitly says not to write flag files or persist anything.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file path appears inside documentation for optional configuration. No write operation, read operation, or filesystem API is present.

low_findings.title: Documentation Link Is Not Network Exfiltration
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The hardcoded URL points to the public project documentation. The skill does not instruct the model to fetch the URL or transmit local data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is labeled as full documentation and contains no dynamic data. No network request code or exfiltration instruction was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The weak-cryptography findings map to frontmatter and headings, not cryptographic code. No hashing algorithm, encryption routine, password handling, or security-sensitive crypto usage appears in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are the description field and a Modes heading. They do not contain crypto primitives or security logic.

content.value_statement: Users need a fast way to remember Caveman modes, commands, and defaults. This skill displays a concise reference card without changing mode or persisting settings.
actual_capabilities: Displays available Caveman modes and their trigger phrases.
Lists related Caveman skills and what each command does.
Shows how to deactivate Caveman mode during a session.
Explains default mode priority using environment and config examples.
Links users to the full public Caveman documentation.
limitations: It is a one-shot help card, not a persistent operating mode.
It does not activate or deactivate Caveman mode by itself.
It does not edit configuration files or environment variables.
It covers only the commands documented in SKILL.md.

use_cases.title: Learn Available Modes
use_cases.description: Review Lite, Full, Ultra, and Wenyan mode triggers before choosing a communication style.

use_cases.title: Find Related Commands
use_cases.description: Check the command names for commit messages, review comments, compression, and help.

use_cases.title: Confirm Default Settings
use_cases.description: See the documented priority order for default mode configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Show Basic Help
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user wants the standard command card.

prompt_templates.title: Ask for Command Options
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants the available Caveman commands in plain language.

prompt_templates.title: Check Mode Triggers
prompt_templates.prompt: what caveman commands switch between lite, full, ultra, and wenyan modes?
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user wants mode names and activation phrases.

prompt_templates.title: Review Configuration Priority
prompt_templates.prompt: show caveman help and include how default mode is resolved
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user wants command help plus configuration order.

output_examples.output: A compact help card listing modes, skill commands, deactivation phrases, default mode configuration, and the documentation link.
A concise command reference that shows each Caveman skill trigger and its purpose.
A short guide showing how to activate modes, stop Caveman mode, and configure the default mode.

best_practices: Use this skill when you need the command list, not when you want to change modes.
Ask for the help card before configuring defaults so the priority order is clear.
Pair the help card with a specific Caveman command when you already know your goal.
anti_patterns: Do not expect this skill to persist mode changes across sessions.
Do not use it as a substitute for the compression, review, or commit skills.
Do not rely on it to modify local config files or environment variables.

faq.question: Does this skill activate Caveman mode?
faq.answer: No. It displays a help card and does not change mode by itself.

faq.question: Can it write a default configuration file?
faq.answer: No. It only shows the documented configuration example.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it include Wenyan modes?
faq.answer: Yes. It lists Wenyan-Lite, Wenyan-Full, and Wenyan-Ultra triggers.

faq.question: Is this a coding skill?
faq.answer: No. It is a productivity reference for Caveman commands and modes.

faq.question: Does it access the network?
faq.answer: No evidence found. SKILL.md only includes a public documentation URL.
